items.title: Safe reads
items.prefer: `d.get(k, default)` or check `k in d` first.

items.title: Hashable, stable keys
items.avoid: Mutable keys or fields that change after insert.
items.why: Keeps `hash(k) == hash(k)` true for the key's lifetime — the dict's core invariant.

items.title: Reach for the right helper
items.avoid: Hand-rolling grouping/counting with `if k in d` boilerplate.
items.prefer: `defaultdict(list)` for grouping, `Counter` for tallies, `setdefault` for inline init.
items.example:
```python
from collections import Counter
freq = Counter(tokens)
top  = freq.most_common(5)
```

items.title: Index, then lookup
items.avoid: Repeatedly scanning a list for the same field.
items.prefer: Build a dict keyed by that field once.
items.why: Turns O(n) repeated scans into O(1) lookups.
